Class SubprojectPruneTransformer

java.lang.Object
network.ike.extension.SubprojectPruneTransformer
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformer, org.apache.maven.api.spi.SpiService
@Named("ike-workspace-subproject-prune") @Singleton public class SubprojectPruneTransformer extends Object implements org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformer
Maven 4 build extension that prunes a workspace POM's <subprojects> and file-activated profiles whose targets are missing from disk, before Maven's model validator gets a chance to fail on "Child subproject X does not exist".

Without this extension, a fresh git clone of an IKE workspace cannot run mvn ws:scaffold-init (or any goal) because the workspace POM declares subprojects that have not yet been cloned into place. Maven 4 reads the POM, sees the declared subproject directories, validates them, and fails before any goal can execute. The <activation><file><exists> guard that earlier workspaces used to gate profile activation does not survive an explicit -P?with-X CLI flag — and the managed -P? block in .mvn/maven.config (added so IntelliJ would see the profiles activated, per IKE-Network/ ike-issues#276) force-activates them regardless. The chicken- and-egg in IKE-Network/ike-issues#459.

This transformer hooks into Maven 4's ModelTransformer.transformFileModel(Model) SPI, which fires before profile activation and subproject existence validation. Two pruning passes are applied to any POM whose directory contains a sibling workspace.yaml (the "this is a workspace POM" marker):

  1. Top-level <subprojects>: each entry whose <subproject-dir>/pom.xml does not exist on disk is removed. This lets workspace POMs declare all subprojects unconditionally at top level, with no with-* profile machinery.
  2. File-activated profiles: each profile whose <activation><file><exists> target does not exist is removed from the model entirely. Profile activation runs after this transformer, sees no such profile, and merges nothing. Any matching -P?with-X flag silently no- ops on a missing profile name. This keeps the transformer backward-compatible with legacy workspaces still using the profile pattern, before migration.

Every other POM (subproject POMs, parent POMs, external consumers) passes through untouched.

The extension is registered in the workspace's .mvn/extensions.xml and loaded by Maven at startup. Its version is managed by ws:scaffold-publish on the IKE platform side; this artifact itself stays at version 1 unless its behavior contract changes.

  • Method Details

    • transformFileModel

      public org.apache.maven.api.model.Model transformFileModel(org.apache.maven.api.model.Model model) throws org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformerException
      Prune missing subprojects and missing-target profiles from the file-stage model. No-op for any POM that lacks a sibling workspace.yaml.
      Specified by:
      transformFileModel in interface org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformer
      Parameters:
      model - the file-stage parsed Maven model
      Returns:
      the (possibly pruned) model — same instance when nothing was pruned
      Throws:
      org.apache.maven.api.spi.ModelTransformerException - never thrown; declared for SPI conformance
